research_goal: Understand why PMs at early-stage startups struggle with discovery research.
personalized_opening: I am especially interested in how your team turns one customer conversation into a sharper next one.

questions.primary: In your last discovery sprint, what specifically changed in the interview guide after the first call?
questions.rationale_gap: Maya said teams keep asking broad questions for too long; this tests whether adaptive question sharpening happens elsewhere.
probes: Was the change based on a quote, a theme, or a stakeholder request?
grounding: Maya: teams keep asking broad workflow questions even by the fourth call

questions.primary: When customer context is scattered across CRM notes, Slack, docs, and support threads, which source actually shapes the next interview?
questions.rationale_gap: Maya identified scattered context as the blocker between interview notes and better follow-up questions.
probes: What information gets ignored because it is hard to find?
What would you need to trust an automated synthesis of those sources?
grounding: Maya: sales notes, support Slack threads, and call docs are not together when writing the next guide

questions.primary: If an agent proposed the next question bank, what evidence would it need to show before you would approve it?
questions.rationale_gap: Maya wants control over direction and evidence, not manual rewriting; this probes the trust threshold.
probes: Would you want to see direct quotes attached to each new question?
grounding: Maya: show me what changed, why it changed, and what evidence it came from
